Class EfsTimerEvent

java.lang.Object
org.efs.timer.EfsTimerEvent
All Implemented Interfaces:
IEfsEvent
@Immutable public final class EfsTimerEvent extends Object implements IEfsEvent
Event reporting a timer expiration. Contains:
  • user-specified timer name which may be null or an empty string.
  • System.nanoTime() timestamp when timer was dispatched to agent. Provided so agent can determine delta between timer dispatchTimestamp and timer event delivery.
Author:
Charles W. Rapp

    Modifier and Type

    datum()
    Returns optional user-provided datum forwarded in this timer.
    long

    Returns when timer event dispatched to agent in Java nanoseconds (see System.nanoTime()).

    Returns true if o is a non-null timer event with the same name and dispatcher timestamp as this timer event.

    Returns hash code based on timer name and dispatch timestamp.
    String

    Returns expired timer's name as configured by agent.

    Returns textual representation of timer event.

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Returns true if o is a non-null timer event with the same name and dispatcher timestamp as this timer event.

      o - comparison object.

      Returns hash code based on timer name and dispatch timestamp.

      Returns textual representation of timer event.

    • timerName

      @Nullable public String timerName()
      Returns expired timer's name as configured by agent. May be null.
      Returns:
      expired timer's name.

    • datum

      @Nullable public Object datum()
      Returns optional user-provided datum forwarded in this timer. May be null
      Returns:
      optional, user-provided datum.

    • dispatchTimestamp

      public long dispatchTimestamp()
      Returns when timer event dispatched to agent in Java nanoseconds (see System.nanoTime()). This value allows agent to determine delay between timer's expiration and delivery to agent. The idea being that an agent may decide to take alternative action if timer delivery was delayed beyond an acceptable time limit.
      Returns:
      timer event dispatch time in nanoseconds.
